prompt: Pharmaceutical product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition, of a clear borosilicate glass vial — 3ml capacity, slim cylindrical body with a narrow neck, exact proportions and silhouette matching the reference image. The vial is sealed with a gray rubber injection stopper crimped under a NAVY BLUE aluminum collar cap, topped with a matching NAVY BLUE plastic flip-off button. The rubber stopper underneath is medical gray. The vial sits centered on a pure clean white seamless studio background with soft even overhead lighting, a subtle natural soft shadow beneath the base, no harsh reflections, neutral white balance. A clean matte-white pharmaceutical label is wrapped around the lower two-thirds of the glass body, hugging the cylinder so it shows a slight curvature on the edges. Label layout from top to bottom: (1) The upper third of the label is completely blank pristine smooth white surface — absolutely no graphics, no text, no boxes, no dashed or dotted lines, no marker shapes, no logo placeholder symbols of any kind, just plain empty white reserved for a future logo. (2) Below that empty zone, a single thin precise NAVY BLUE horizontal hairline rule (matching the cap color exactly) runs the full width of the label. (3) Below that hairline, the product name 'BPC-157' is centered in clean modern medical sans-serif typography, dark charcoal grey, with refined slight letter-spacing for a premium feel, crisp and sharp. (4) Below the product name, a second matching thin NAVY BLUE hairline rule (same color as cap) runs the full width of the label. (5) Along the very bottom edge of the label, a slim solid NAVY BLUE accent bar (matching the cap color, roughly 6 percent of the label height) runs edge to edge, giving the label a finished anchored feel and visually tying the label to the cap color. The hairlines and bottom bar are all the SAME navy blue as the cap. The product name remains in grey, NOT navy. No other text, icons, decorations, or graphics anywhere on the label. Style: clinical, minimalist, premium pharmaceutical, modern medical packaging, refined typographic hierarchy with coordinated cap-color accents, commercial product shot, sharp focus, high resolution.
